context: Tests whether react-ban-use-effect keeps user-caused work in handlers and replaces identity-change reset effects with component boundaries or equivalent declarative reset semantics.

checklist.name: No direct useEffect
checklist.description: The refactored code does not import `useEffect`, call `useEffect`, or call `React.useEffect`

checklist.name: Submit in handler
checklist.description: The save request is triggered directly from the click or submit handler, not via a state flag observed by an effect

checklist.name: Flag state removed
checklist.description: `shouldSubmit` or equivalent effect-trigger flag state is removed

checklist.name: Reset semantics declarative
checklist.description: Changing `user.id` resets draft state with a keyed child component, keyed boundary, or a clearly guarded render-time alternative rather than an effect

checklist.name: Draft editing preserved
checklist.description: The user can still edit the input locally before saving

checklist.name: Scope controlled
checklist.description: The solution does not rewrite unrelated form architecture or introduce a new form/data dependency without being asked

checklist.name: Categories explained
checklist.description: The report classifies the original effects as event-caused work and identity-change reset

checklist.name: Verification named
checklist.description: The report names lint/type/test checks and a small interaction check for editing, saving, and switching users
